_source: 6.3 CAPABILITY POINT EXPENDITURE SUMMARY (Charts & Tables Common to Both Players)
_note: Transcribed verbatim from the 6.3 chart. The combat engine wires the assault/defend and break-contact/disengage rows; the probe and undergo-barrage rows are transcribed for the record but not yet consumed (the engine does not model a distinct Probe). Terrain/minefield (TEC/CPA formulae), load/unload, construction, paradrop and air-readiness rows are out of scope here -- movement already charges its own CP via UNIT_MOVED.cp_spent.
_organization_note: The four ORGANIZATION rows of the same 6.3 chart (docs/rules/90-charts-tables-and-play-aids.md:529-532), wired by rule 19 (game.organization). Each is charged to BOTH counters named in its cell -- the Parent Formation and the subsidiary unit -- which is what the parenthesis says and what [19.41]/[19.42]/[19.43]/[19.44] repeat in prose ('a cost of one Capability Point each to both units').
